title: Mailchimp vs ActiveCampaign (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Mailchimp and ActiveCampaign. See pricing, features, and which email marketing tool fits your business needs.
intro: Mailchimp and ActiveCampaign are both popular email marketing platforms, but they serve different business needs. Mailchimp excels as an accessible, beginner-friendly option with a free tier, while ActiveCampaign targets growing businesses requiring advanced automation and CRM capabilities. This comparison will help you determine which platform aligns best with your marketing goals and budget.

comparison_table.tool_a: Drag-drop editor, A/B testing, landing pages
comparison_table.tool_b: Advanced automation, CRM, lead scoring, SMS

choose_a_if: You're just starting with email marketing and need a free option
You want an intuitive drag-and-drop editor and straightforward interface
You're a small business with a limited budget and up to 500 subscribers
choose_b_if: You need advanced automation workflows and lead scoring capabilities
You want integrated CRM functionality for better customer relationship management
You require SMS marketing, site tracking, and dynamic content personalization

faq.a: Neither is universally better—it depends on your needs. Mailchimp is superior for beginners and small businesses due to its free tier and ease of use. ActiveCampaign excels for growing businesses needing advanced automation, CRM integration, and sophisticated segmentation. Choose based on your business stage and feature requirements.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Mailchimp or ActiveCampaign?
faq.a: Mailchimp is cheaper for small businesses because it offers a free tier for up to 500 subscribers and paid plans starting at $13/month. ActiveCampaign has no free tier and starts at $15/month. However, ActiveCampaign may provide better value for larger operations needing advanced features.

faq.q: Can I switch from ActiveCampaign to Mailchimp?
faq.a: Yes, you can migrate your email lists and contacts from ActiveCampaign to Mailchimp, though you may lose some advanced automation data during the transfer. Both platforms support CSV exports and integrations that facilitate data migration. Plan the transition carefully to minimize disruption to your campaigns.
